ticket_title: BE - Split ValidateDevice activity into 4 focused activities

test_cases.name: TC-1: Standard Handset routes ValidateDevice → ResolveDevicePricing (no collection step) [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: RTGO Backend with feature/RTPL-1925 deployed; Handset in Dealer inventory; no EDGE/BYOD/EID/HINT conditions apply. | Test Data: deviceType = Handset, inventoryType = D (ASTOCK), storeType = COR, role = Store Rep

steps.action: Initiate a new activation for a standard Handset (ASTOCK Dealer).
Complete ValidateDevice; confirm no routing flags are set (NeedsEdgeCreation=false, NeedsByodCreation=false, NeedsEid=false, IsHintDevice=false).
Observe the next activity returned by the process engine.
Confirm ResolveDevicePricing executes and produces SIM options and pricing data.
Confirm ValidateSimCard is the subsequent activity.
steps.expected: Routing bypasses CollectDeviceInfo and ValidateHint; pricing resolves correctly; ValidateSimCard is next. No DeviceStateNotAvailableException thrown.

test_cases.name: TC-2: BYOD device needing EDGE creation routes through CollectDeviceInfo [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: BYOD Handset not in local POS inventory; NeedsEdgeCreation=true expected from ValidateDevice. | Test Data: deviceType = Handset, inventoryType = Byod (BYOD), storeType = COR, role = Store Rep, IMEI = not in local inventory

steps.action: Initiate activation for a BYOD Handset not in local inventory.
Complete ValidateDevice; confirm NeedsEdgeCreation=true is set in DeviceState before activity completes.
Confirm next activity is CollectDeviceInfo (not ResolveDevicePricing).
Submit accept-add-to-edge action in CollectDeviceInfo; confirm EDGE creation call succeeds.
Confirm routing proceeds to ResolveDevicePricing then ValidateSimCard.
steps.expected: ValidateDevice sets NeedsEdgeCreation=true; CollectDeviceInfo is visited; device is added to EDGE with local inventory update; pricing resolves; ValidateSimCard follows.

test_cases.name: TC-3: BYOD decline-EDGE → BYOD fallback offered within CollectDeviceInfo [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: BYOD Handset; rep declines adding to EDGE. | Test Data: deviceType = Handset, inventoryType = Byod, action = decline-add-to-edge, storeType = COR, role = Store Rep

steps.action: Initiate BYOD activation; ValidateDevice sets NeedsEdgeCreation=true.
In CollectDeviceInfo, submit decline-add-to-edge action.
Confirm accept-add-as-byod action is now available (BYOD creation offer).
Submit accept-add-as-byod; confirm BYOD device creation succeeds.
Confirm routing proceeds to ResolveDevicePricing.
steps.expected: Declining EDGE immediately surfaces BYOD creation within the same CollectDeviceInfo activity; BYOD device is created; pricing resolves correctly.

test_cases.name: TC-4: HINT (HomeInternet) device routes ValidateDevice → ValidateHint → ResolveDevicePricing [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: HINT (HomeInternet) device available in store inventory; address validation service accessible. | Test Data: deviceType = HomeInternet (HINT), storeType = COR, role = Store Rep, inventoryType = D (ASTOCK)

steps.action: Initiate activation for a HINT device.
Complete ValidateDevice; confirm IsHintDevice=true is set in DeviceState.
Confirm next activity is ValidateHint (not CollectDeviceInfo).
Provide address and consent in ValidateHint; confirm address validation and eligibility check execute.
Confirm EligibilityId is stored and routing proceeds to ResolveDevicePricing.
steps.expected: IsHintDevice=true routes to ValidateHint; address/eligibility/consent are collected; EligibilityId is stored; pricing resolves; ValidateSimCard follows.

test_cases.name: TC-5: DeviceState rename — no NullReferenceException across activation paths [Priority: Medium]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Backend deployed with feature/RTPL-1925; Wearable IMEI available. | Test Data: deviceType = Wearable, inventoryType = D (ASTOCK), storeType = ARC, role = Store Rep

steps.action: Initiate Wearable activation.
Complete ValidateDevice and ResolveDevicePricing.
Inspect process state for DeviceState presence and property accessibility.
Confirm all DeviceState properties (Stage, routing flags, DeviceType) are readable without error.
steps.expected: DeviceState is populated and accessible; no NullReferenceException or DeviceStateNotAvailableException thrown.

test_cases.name: TC-6: Legacy CosmosDB event (pre-split) deserializes and resumes correctly after deploy [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: CosmosDB test instance contains a persisted activation event with pre-RTPL-1925 DeviceValidationDetails schema; process paused at old ValidateDevice activity. | Test Data: Legacy event JSON: DeviceValidationDetails with pre-split field names; process state paused at ValidateDevice

steps.action: Deploy feature/RTPL-1925 to test environment.
Load a persisted in-flight activation with the legacy event schema.
Resume the activation process.
Confirm DeviceState is populated from old field names without error.
Confirm process continues without deserialization error.
steps.expected: Legacy event deserializes without error; DeviceState populated correctly from old schema; process advances to the next activity.
